entries.title: Pythagorean Theorem
entries.content: In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides. Only applies to right triangles (one 90° angle).

entries.title: Area of a Circle
entries.content: The area enclosed by a circle is proportional to the square of its radius. Pi (π) is approximately 3.14159, the ratio of circumference to diameter.

entries.title: Volume of a Sphere
entries.content: Three-dimensional measure of space inside a sphere. Related to surface area formula; both involve π and radius.

entries.title: Sum of Interior Angles (Polygon)
entries.content: The sum of interior angles of any n-sided convex polygon is (n-2)×180°. For triangles n=3 gives 180°; quadrilateral gives 360°.

entries.title: Similar Triangles
entries.content: Triangles with equal corresponding angles and proportional sides. Ratio of corresponding sides is constant (scale factor). Used in indirect measurement and proofs.
